data.title: Bank of Japan to Offload Stock Holdings in Further Policy Normalization—Update
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

TOKYO--The Bank of Japan said it will begin selling its stock holdings, a move seen as a step further in monetary policy normalization and a sign of confidence in the economy.

The central bank on Friday left its policy rate unchanged and announced plans to reduce its holdings of exchange-traded funds, which it had purchased under its aggressive monetary-easing program aimed at tackling deflation.

It said it would offload about 330 billion yen, or $2.23 billion, in ETF holdings annually, based on book value.

"This marks a significant and symbolic step away from the ultra-loose policies of the Abenomics era," said StoneX senior market analyst Matt Simpson, referring to pro-growth policies pushed by former Prime Minister Shinzo Abe.

"The key takeaway is that the BOJ has officially begun the process of reducing its unconventional asset holdings," Simpson said.

Japanese stocks fell following the decision, with the Nikkei Stock Average declining as much as 1.8% before closing 0.6% lower. The index has recently set record highs on expectations of new political leadership at home and easing concerns over the U.S. economic outlook.

The central bank said it may adjust the pace of ETF sales at future meetings and pledged to minimize losses and financial-market disruption.

It also plans to sell about Y5 billion in real-estate investment trust holdings.

Planned annual sales of ETFs and J-REITs amount to about 0.05% of market trading volume, according to the central bank. As of Sept. 10, it held approximately Y37.2 trillion of ETFs.

Economists at Daiwa Securities noted the "extremely gradual" pace of sales: "If this scale continues, it would take more than 100 years to fully sell off the portfolio."

"The details are clearly designed to be considerate of the market," they said.

The BOJ kept its overnight call rate target steady at 0.5%, where it has been since its January rate increase, as it assesses U.S. tariffs and domestic political uncertainty.

While a trade agreement between the U.S. and Japan cleared some doubts, the economic and corporate impact remains unclear. Although Japan secured a lower tariff rate than Washington had initially threatened, companies still face higher costs than before, which could erode competitiveness in the U.S. market and hurt profits.

The BOJ is watching whether companies maintain capital investment and pay raises under the new tariff regime. Key indicators include the upcoming tankan corporate sentiment survey and the quarterly BOJ branch managers meeting, which tracks regional economic conditions and wage growth momentum.

Economists remain divided on the outlook. Some expect the BOJ to hold off on rate hikes until next year, while others say it can make a move as soon as October if it becomes confident that prices and wages are on track to keep growing at a healthy pace.

Two BOJ policy board members, Hajime Takata and Naoki Tamura, dissented from Friday's decision to hold, signaling they believe the central bank is ready for another increase.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: Factors to watch on September 19
data.description:
Britain's FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 index is seen opening higher on Friday, with futures up 0.1%.

* IG GROUP: British online trading platform IG Group said on Friday it had acquired Australian cryptocurrency exchange Independent Reserve for A$178 million ($117.4 million), expanding its digital offerings and presence in the Asia-Pacific region.

* UK-BASED INIGO: Radian Group said on Thursday it would buy UK-based Inigo for $1.7 billion as the U.S. mortgage insurer seeks to transform into a global multi-line specialty insurer, sending its shares up nearly 7%.

* SPIRE HEALTHCARE: British private hospital group Spire Healthcare said on Thursday it had initiated discussions with several parties to explore strategic options, including a potential sale of the company.

* CONSUMERS: Britons became more gloomy this month and the prospect of tax increases in finance minister Rachel Reeves' budget in November threatens to damage confidence levels further, a survey showed on Friday.

* RETAIL: British retail sales rose by a stronger-than-expected 0.5% in August, helped by sunny weather, but sales growth in July was revised slightly down, official figures showed on Friday.

* BORROWING: Britain's borrowing has surged past the official forecasts that underpin the government's tax and spending plans, compounding the challenge facing finance minister Rachel Reeves in her November budget.

* COMMODITIES: Oil prices were little changed on Friday, gold prices were flat and copper futures rebounded from a one-week low hit in the previous session.

* For more on the factors affecting European stocks, please click on:

TODAY'S UK PAPERS

> Financial Times

> Other business headlines

data.title: Iron Ore Steadies on Improving Demand
data.description:
Iron ore futures held near CNY 805 per ton on Friday and were on track for a weekly gain as demand for the key steelmaking input showed signs of improvement.

Industry data from China indicated that hot metal output, a gauge of iron ore consumption, exceeded 2.4 million tons this week, while blast furnace capacity utilization climbed above 90%.

Restocking ahead of the National Day holiday also supported the ferrous market, alongside seasonal demand from infrastructure projects.

Still, rising overseas shipments and a recovery in domestic supply could cap further gains.

In addition, sentiment was dampened by a lack of policy support, as the People’s Bank of China kept a key rate unchanged and showed little urgency to ease monetary settings.

data.title: South African Markets - Factors to watch on September 19
data.description:
The following scheduled economic events, equity and currency market moves may affect South African markets on Friday.

ECONOMIC EVENTS

Inflation-linked bond auction

SOUTH AFRICAN MARKETS

The South African rand was largely flat after the central bank kept interest rates unchanged as it cautiously monitored the effect of previous cuts.

On the Johannesburg Stock Exchange, the Top-40 index was also little changed.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian shares were headed for weekly gains on Friday on hopes of further rate cuts around the world, while the Nikkei retreated from record highs after the Bank of Japan flagged a further unwinding of its massive stimulus policies.

WALL STREET

Wall Street's main indexes posted record-high closes on Thursday, a day after the U.S. Federal Reserve delivered a quarter-point interest rate cut, while chipmaker Intel rose after Nvidia decided to build a stake in the company.

GOLD

Gold rose on Friday and headed for a fifth straight weekly gain after the U.S. Federal Reserve cut interest rates by 25 basis points this week and signalled a gradual easing path for the rest of the year.

EMERGING MARKETS

For the top emerging markets news, double click on

NEWS24

- G20 talks with U.S., Argentina ‘difficult’, but new agri markets on the horizon —South Africa's agricultural minister

data.title: Stuti Jhawar Hikes Stake in Usha Martin by 15,000 Shares
data.description:
Stuti Jhawar increased her stake in Usha Martin Ltd by purchasing 15,000 equity shares through an open market transaction on the National Stock Exchange (NSE) on September 18, 2025. Following this acquisition, Jhawar's holding in Usha Martin has risen from 12,60,000 shares to 12,75,000 shares, representing 0.42% of the total equity.

Prior to the acquisition, Jhawar held 12,60,000 shares, which constituted 0.41% of the company's equity. The shares were acquired through the National Stock Exchange (NSE) - Open Market.

data.title: Spire Healthcare Hangs Up 'For Sale' Sign; Not in Any Takeover Talks
data.description:
(06:07 GMT) *Spire Healthcare Working With Advisors Over Potential Sale of Co
(06:07 GMT) *Spire Healthcare: Process Is Highly Preliminary; No Decision Has Been Made
(06:08 GMT) *Spire Healthcare: No Certainty Either That Any Offer Will Be Made
(06:08 GMT) *Spire Healthcare Isn't in Receipt of Any Approaches
(06:08 GMT) *Spire Healthcare Isn't in Talks With Any Parties in Respect of a Potential Sale
(06:09 GMT) *Spire Healthcare Started Process to Hold Talks With Parties Over Potential Options
(06:46 GMT) Spire Healthcare Hangs Up 'For Sale' Sign; Not in Any Takeover Talks

By Billy Gray

Spire Healthcare said it started talks with a number of parties over options for the business to drive long term shareholder growth, including a potential sale of the group.

The U.K. private hospital company said Friday that it was working with its advisor Rothschild & Co over options and that the process is highly preliminary, with no decision yet made over potential options.

The company said it hasn't received any takeover approaches or been in talks with possible buyers for the business.

The Financial Times reported that the company had hired Rothschild over a potential sale of the company for up to 3.40 pounds a share that would value it at 1.4 billion pounds ($1.90 billion).

Citing unnamed sources, the FT said that investors, including the Toscafund hedge fund and investment manager Harwood Capital, along with its activist trust Achilles, put pressure on the company to put itself up for sale.

Toscafund owns 18.16% of the company's issued share capital, while Harwood owns 4.07%, according to LSEG.

Harwood and Toscafund declined to comment to the Financial Times.

Shares closed Thursday at 216.50 pence.

Write to Billy Gray at william.gray@wsj.com

data.title: Australian shares end higher on rate cut bets; banks, healthcare lead gains
data.description:
Key points:
  • ASX 200 posts third straight weekly loss
  • Telix and Pro Medicus surge after positive Citi ratings
  • Gold stocks snap six-week winning streak

By Sneha Kumar

Australian shares ended higher on Friday, supported by gains in banking and healthcare stocks, a day after a weak labour market report boosted expectations of a rate cut in November.

The S&P/ASX 200 benchmark index closed 0.3% higher at 8,773.50 points. It ended the week down 1%, marking a third week of losses.

Financials rose 0.6%, benefiting from expectations of increased lending volumes that typically follow a lower interest rate environment. Three of the "Big Four" banks ended higher while Westpac slid 0.3%.

Thursday's soft employment data bolstered bets of a rate cut in November after a widely-expected hold by the Reserve Bank of Australia's in its meeting on September 30.

"Essentially traders are looking beyond the potential rates-hold this month and are focusing on prospects that rates could be headed lower in November," said Tim Waterer, chief market analyst at KCM Trade.

Meanwhile, healthcare stocks climbed 0.9% and were steered by Telix Pharmaceuticals , which emerged as one of the top performers on the broader benchmark.

Telix rose 6.4% after Citi initiated stock coverage with a "buy" rating, while an upgrade to a "buy" rating from "sell" propelled software group Pro Medicus' shares by 4.9%.

Analysts said an overnight rally in the U.S. equities provided a positive lead to local equities, with domestic tech stocks surging to a record during the session before giving up some gains to end 0.9% higher. ASX-listed Xero finished up 1.4%.

Gold stocks tracked bullion to climb 0.8%, with Northern Star Resources and Evolution Mining adding 1.2% and 3.2% respectively. Yet, the sub-index logged a weekly loss of 2.6%, snapping a six-week winning streak.

Mining stocks dipped 0.2%, with BHP , Rio Tinto and Fortescue falling in a range of 0.2-1.3%.

New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index rose 0.9% to 13,231.66 points.

data.title: CESC to back subsidiary’s Rs 5,000 crore solar and battery manufacturing project
data.description:
CESC Ltd has announced that it will provide financial support to its wholly owned subsidiary, CESC Green Power Ltd, for setting up a large-scale renewable energy and manufacturing project with a planned capital investment of up to Rs 5,000 crore.

According to the company’s disclosure, the project will include the establishment of a solar cell and module manufacturing facility with a capacity of over 3 GW, a battery manufacturing unit, and a 60 MW renewable energy power plant across various locations in India. The support from CESC will cover equity infusion, strategic assistance, and facilitation of institutional funding to enable financial closure and smooth implementation of the project.

The company said this move aligns with Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015.

This project comes as part of CESC’s broader strategy to accelerate its clean energy portfolio. The company, through its renewable arm Purvah Green Power, has outlined plans to build a clean energy pipeline of around 10 GW, with 3.2 GW of hybrid wind and solar projects expected to be commissioned by FY29. CESC has also awarded a Rs 346 crore EPC contract to Waaree Renewable Technologies for a 300 MW AC solar project in Rajasthan, and Purvah Green is executing a 150 MW wind-solar hybrid project as part of its expanding portfolio.

Industry experts note that favourable policy developments -- including recent GST cuts on renewable energy equipment -- are expected to lower capital costs and improve project viability, providing a supportive backdrop for CESC’s large-scale green power push.Shares of CESC were trading at Rs 166.09 on Friday, up 0.66 percent within a day’s range of Rs 164.67-167.35. The stock has declined 12.22 percent over the past year and has traded between Rs 119 and Rs 212.49 during the period.

data.title: South Korea trying to resolve issues with US auto tariffs quickly, trade envoy says
data.description:
South Korea's top trade envoy said on Friday that South Korea is trying to resolve issues around U.S. auto tariffs quickly to avoid being disadvantaged by higher duties, explaining to the U.S. that "Japan and South Korea are different".

Japan and South Korea are leading car exporters but under a trade deal with Japan the U.S. applies a 15% tariff on Japanese car imports, while U.S. tariffs on South Korean auto imports are 25%.

On July 30, President Donald Trump said the U.S. will reduce duties on automobile imports to 15% in return for Seoul investing $350 billion in the U.S, but the change has yet to be implemented as the countries remain at odds over the details of the investments.

"We've explained the differences between Japan and Korea as much as possible," Trade envoy Yeo Han-koo told reporters upon his return on Friday from a trip to Washington this week where he met with U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer.

Top officials in Seoul have repeated in recent days that accepting all U.S. demands and copying the format of Japan's deal with the U.S. would be difficult due to issues such as the domestic foreign exchange implications of the investment fund.

"Disagreements have increased in the process of discussing details since (the meeting between Trump and South Korean President Lee Jae Myung in August), and now (tariff talks) are in a difficult state," National Security Adviser Wi Sung-lac told reporters on Friday. "But we shouldn't lose our whole relationship while talking money."

Lee will visit New York next week to attend the UN General Assembly, Wi said. During his trip, the president will meet with Wall Street heavyweights including BlackRock CEO Larry Fink to discuss artificial intelligence, energy and the South Korean economy as an investment destination, but tariff talks will be conducted separately from Lee's trip, he said.

Foreign Minister Cho Hyun told a briefing on Friday that the government would try to resolve visa issues facing Korean workers in the U.S. before proceeding with the $350 billion investment package.

His remarks come after hundreds of Korean workers were recently detained during a U.S. immigration a raid on a Hyundai Motor battery plant in the state of Georgia, prompting calls by Seoul for a new category of visa for skilled Korean workers.

data.title: India's HPCL buys 2 million barrels of West African crude for end-October arrival
data.description:
Hindustan Petroleum Corp has bought 2 million barrels of West African crude for end-October delivery, trade sources said on Friday.

The cargo of 1 million barrels each of Nigerian Bonny Light and Angolan Pazflor was sold by Shell, they said.

The price was not immediately available.

( the "Company")

Net Asset Value

The Company announces that, at its close of business on 18 September 2025 its unaudited net asset value was USD 2004.2 million or USD 12.05 per share (Sterling equivalent at that date was GBP 1478.8 million or GBP 8.89 per share).

More information on the Company is available at

data.title: Kancera To Seek Stakeholders For Reverse Take Over
data.description:
Kancera AB :

  • SAID ON THURSDAY KANCERA INITIATES PROCESS TO SEEK STAKEHOLDERS FOR REVERSE TAKE OVER

  • AIM IS TO MATERIALIZE VALUE OF CO'S PUBLIC LISTING

  • OPPORTUNITIES TO RAISE SUFFICIENT AMOUNT OF EXTERNAL CAPITAL NEAR-TERM IS PERCEIVED TO BE LOW

  • SURVIVAL OF COMPANY IS PERCEIVED TO BE AT RISK, IF SIGNIFICANT MEASURES ARE NOT TAKEN

  • IF THERE IS LACK OF STRATEGIC OPTIONS, BOARD’S INTENTION IS TO RECOMMEND DE-LISTING FROM NASDAQ FIRST NORTH GROWTH MARKET AND VOLUNTARY LIQUIDATION

  • DECISION TO EXECUTE A POTENTIAL REVERSE TAKE OVER, DE-LISTING OR LIQUIDATION WILL BE TAKEN AT FUTURE GENERAL MEETING

Source text for Workspace:
